steps.title: Identify two clear points on the line of best fit
steps.content: Examine the line of best fit drawn through the scatterplot. The line slopes gently upward from left to right. Reading carefully, the line passes through approximately $(0, 2.5)$ and $(10, 4.5)$.

steps.title: Apply the slope formula
steps.content:
Use the slope formula with these two points:
$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{4.5 - 2.5}{10 - 0} = \frac{2}{10} = 0.2$$
Checking another pair of points on the line ($x = 2$, $y \approx 2.9$ and $x = 8$, $y \approx 4.3$):
$$m = \frac{4.3 - 2.9}{8 - 2} = \frac{1.4}{6} \approx 0.233$$
Both estimates give a slope close to $0.22$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-2.45$: A steep negative slope — the line clearly rises (positive slope), so this is eliminated.
  • B) $-0.22$: Negative sign is wrong; the line rises, not falls.
  • C) $0.22$: Matches our calculated slope of roughly $0.2$ to $0.23$.
  • D) $2.45$: Far too steep; a slope of $2.45$ would rise much faster than what is shown.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
